title: 8-Step Problem Solving A3
purpose: Identify a problem and take logical steps to a desired solution. Prevent problems before they occur, resolve recurring errors, reduce the impact of each incident.

scoringMatrix.note: Score each countermeasure 0/1/2 per dimension. Overall (OA) is the ranked priority, not a simple sum.

methods.genchiGenbutsu: Go and see for yourself — practical experience over theoretical knowledge. Used in Steps 2 & 4.
methods.nemawashi: Preliminary work to involve other sections/departments — build consensus before committing to a countermeasure. Used in Step 5.
methods.yokoten: Share the countermeasure with other processes and locations that could apply it. Used in Step 8.

odgGate.rule: Step 6 is an ODG gate — the countermeasure plan is submitted to ODG for review before implementation proceeds. Statuses: pending → submitted → approved / changes-requested.

sopWriteBack.rule: Step 8 writes the improvement back to the SOP library (Standard Work). SOPs are the INPUT to Steps 1–5 and the OUTPUT of Step 8 (Yokoten).

steps.name: Clarify the Problem
steps.description: Visualize current vs standard — the gap is the problem. Key words: REDUCE, IMPROVE, ENSURE.

fields.label: Ultimate Goal
fields.hint: High-level KPI assurance statement to improve/reduce/ensure.

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: The current standard. If none exists, you must create one.

fields.label: Current Situation
fields.hint: What is actually happening right now.

fields.label: Gap = Problem
fields.hint: The difference between current situation and standard — show it in a callout.

fields.label: Chart
fields.hint: Visual of the gap over time (actual vs target).

steps.name: Break Down the Problem
steps.description: Systematic breakdown large→small. Answer what/where/when/who (NOT why). Genchi Genbutsu to the point of occurrence.

fields.label: Stratification (what / where / when / who)
fields.hint: Break the gap into smaller problems by location, period, job type, product line, team.

fields.label: Prioritized Problem at point of occurrence
fields.hint: Narrow to a single prioritized problem at the point where the gap originates.

steps.name: Target Setting (Objective)
steps.description: Set measurable, concrete, challenging targets. Key word: ELIMINATE the prioritized problem at point of occurrence.

fields.label: Do What?
fields.hint: The action verb and subject — ELIMINATE the prioritized problem.

fields.label: To What?
fields.hint: The target value / condition.

fields.label: By When?
fields.hint: Target date.

fields.label: Chart
fields.hint: Elimination of prioritized problem relative to the big-problem gap.

steps.name: Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: At the point of occurrence, fact-based (no assumptions, no blaming). Keep asking Why until a single root cause. 6M fishbone.

fields.label: Why 1
fields.hint: Directly observable symptom — what you can see happening.

fields.label: Why 2
fields.hint: Why did Why 1 occur?

fields.label: Why 3
fields.hint: Why did Why 2 occur?

fields.label: Why 4
fields.hint: Why did Why 3 occur?

fields.label: Why 5
fields.hint: Why did Why 4 occur? (root-cause level)

fields.label: Root Cause (confirmed)
fields.hint: The single systemic root cause supported by the 5-Whys and 6M.

steps.name: Develop Countermeasures
steps.description: Brainstorm many; narrow to the most practical/effective (risk & difficulty). Build consensus (Nemawashi). Score each on the matrix.

fields.label: Countermeasure Scoring Matrix
fields.hint: One row per candidate; score Safety/Quality/Cost/Time/Customer/Effective 0–2, then set Overall priority.

steps.name: See Countermeasures Through (Implement)
steps.description: Implement countermeasures one by one. Submit the plan to the ODG gate before implementing. Track with R-Y-G-C.

fields.label: Action Register
fields.hint: No. | Implementation Plan | Start Date | Due Date | Responsible | Status (R=Behind / Y=At Risk / G=On Track / C=Completed). Up to 10 rows.

steps.name: Monitor Results & Processes
steps.description: Evaluate result + process from three viewpoints (Customer / World Emblem / your own). If failure → return to Step 5.

fields.label: KPI
fields.hint: The KPI being tracked.

fields.label: Measurement — Start (baseline)
fields.hint: Baseline actual when problem was confirmed.

fields.label: Measurement — End (result)
fields.hint: Actual after implementing countermeasures.

fields.label: New Target
fields.hint: Revised target if the countermeasure redefined the baseline.

fields.label: Result Narrative
fields.hint: What happened, and why success/failure.

fields.label: Chart — Actual vs Target
fields.hint: KPI trajectory before and after implementation.

steps.name: Standardize Successful Process
steps.description: Standardize the new process and Yokoten it. SOPs are the OUTPUT of this step — write the improvement back to the SOP library.

fields.label: Process Documents Created/Updated
fields.hint: Every BWI/SWI/SOP created or updated. Link to the WMS SW Update Request ticket.

fields.label: Training on New Process(es) — Who & When
fields.hint: Who was trained and when (use the Training Log Sheet, RH-FR-6.2-001).

fields.label: Yokoten Plan
fields.hint: Communicate in-plant and to other plants/processes that could apply the countermeasure.

fields.label: Improvement Image or Chart
fields.hint: Before/after visual confirming the process change.
